title: feat: store chunk content + language/app/git_sha in Vectorize metadata
author: Itachi-1824
pr_body_excerpt:
Vectorize has no equivalent to ChromaDB's `documents` field, so search results came back as coordinates — file path and line range, no code. Anything wanting the actual text had to go fetch it separately.

Now the chunk's text rides along in the metadata, plus `language`, `app` (top-level monorepo dir), and `git_sha`.

Metadata is capped at 10KiB/vector, so chunks shrank to fit: 50 lines / 2000 tokens instead of 100 / 8000. Worst case measures ~3KB, well inside the budget.

Also swapped the 200KB file-size skip for a content check. Size was the wrong signal — it was dropping `apps/APPS.md` alongside the build artifacts it was aimed at. Now it skips on average line length (minified bundles run 1,900–131,000 chars/line) and generated-file banners. Three bundles skipped, 1,858 files indexed.

Index is `polli-code-embeddings` — needs a manual `full` run after merge.

gist.headline: Code search gets the code
gist.blurb: The hive stopped returning map coordinates when someone asked for the actual flower. Indexed code chunks now carry their own text, language, app, and commit trail.
gist.summary: Updated the polli-code-embeddings Vectorize index so each code chunk stores its content, language, top-level app directory, and git SHA in metadata, while resizing chunks to 50 lines or 2,000 tokens to stay under Vectorize's 10 KiB metadata limit.
gist.impact: Internal code-search results can now return the indexed code directly instead of only a file path and line range, and the index keeps useful files such as apps/APPS.md while filtering minified and generated bundles by content.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy code garden represents the embeddings index: Polly, wearing tiny librarian glasses, carefully packs glowing code snippets into labeled seed packets marked content, language, app, and git SHA. A helpful CRT monitor robot reads the snippets directly while a few tangled, extra-long minified-code vines are politely sorted into a compost bin.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot wearing tiny librarian glasses, placing glowing code snippets into lime green (#ecf874) seed packets labeled in simple pixel text: 'content', 'language', 'app', and 'git SHA'; a cute CRT monitor robot happily reads the packets beside a garden of code-plants, while tangled long-line code vines go into a small compost bin. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, emotionally warm and nostalgic.
